# Spec context: §3.1 — Description of information fields

## Preceding prose

> The `L:` field specifies the unit note length - the length of a note as represented by a single letter in abc - see [note lengths](#note_lengths "abc:standard:v2.1 ↵") for more details.
> Commonly used values for unit note length are `L:1/4` - quarter note (crotchet), `L:1/8` - eighth note (quaver) and `L:1/16` - sixteenth note (semi-quaver). `L:1` (whole note) - or equivalently `L:1/1`, `L:1/2` (minim), `L:1/32` (demi-semi-quaver), `L:1/64`, `L:1/128`, `L:1/256` and `L:1/512` are al
> If there is no `L:` field defined, a unit note length is set by default, based on the meter field `M:`. This default is calculated by computing the meter as a decimal: if it is less than 0.75 the default unit note length is a sixteenth note; if it is 0.75 or greater, it is an eighth note. For exampl
> A meter change within the body of the tune will not change the unit note length.
> The `Q:` field defines the tempo in terms of a number of beats per minute, e.g. `Q:1/2=120` means 120 half-note beats per minute.
> There may be up to 4 beats in the definition, e.g:
